column18:
Town Hall Meeting Survey
Informed Consent

Hello Chico Student:

The following online survey is part of an important university study on how to improve students' experiences at the Town Hall Meeting. It will take approximately 5-10 minutes to complete.

Taking part in this research is completely voluntary. If you choose to participate in this research, it is very important that you answer the questions as thoughtfully and frankly as possible. All your answers will be kept strictly confidential. You can stop participating at any time without penalty to your grade.
We also emphasize that you will NOT be penalized if you choose to omit answers to some questions on the survey. To record your answers, you must proceed all the way through the end of the survey, even if you choose to omit answers to some questions. In addition, proceeding to the very end of the survey will remove any survey response information from the screen on your computer/device, protecting your privacy. 

Your student ID is being collected so we can match your responses to other important university programs (U-Course, Town Hall Meeting, Great Debate) and demographic information. IDs may be used for statistical analysis alongside other data such as GPA, retention, and graduation rates. Again, your answers are strictly confidential, and we are bound by the rules of this University to maintain this confidentiality. Your information collected as part of this research will be stored on a password-protected computer in a locked office within the First-Year Experience Program office and on a protected server that is located behind a secure firewall at the University Office for Institutional Research. You must be 18 years of age or older to take this survey.

Thank you for filling out this survey as completely and honestly as you can. We are asking you to do this because we value your experience and ideas about how to improve students' experiences here at Chico State.

If you'd like more information about this project, contact the First-Year Experience office at fye@csuchico.edu or (530) 898-3705.

column58: I felt like the only time it was meaningful was when I had the chance to talk to someone else more deeply. The breakout rooms we're not a good place for that. It took to long going through everyones topics and not enough time at the end to fully talked, plus it was cramped
column59: The Town Hall Meeting was honestly disappointing. Going into the room, the person standing outside gave me 0 directions whatsoever. When I got in it took me a moment to realize what I had to do, getting a lanyard and stuff. But the information was on a projected presentation that would change slides and info every 30 seconds. Very unhelpful. Plus, when we finally got to doing the meeting, I felt like it took to long going through each and every persons topic. Plus, having one expert was not helpful. He was helpful on the subjects he knew about, but obviously couldn't contribute to the things he had no knowledge about. And, for the second half, it felt like we we're given the option to talk about our solutions or interviews. It was not very concrete what we we're supposed to do. And, the discussion after was difficult with the cramped space, making it difficult to hear my peer. Finally, after that ended and I had to go to the closing conversation, I went to the Meriam library for the sensory space, but there were no signs anywhere. I had no clue where I was supposed to go. So, I had to go everywhere to ask for help, then I had to go to the BMU, found out where it was in the library, ran back to the library, and found the sensory space, but I was the only student there. No other students showed up. But, I did enjoy my conversation with one teacher. It was definitely the biggest highlight of the night and I am grateful I talked to her. But everything else felt weird and unorganized.
